summary: The passage discusses various symbolic and cryptographic devices used in antiquity, including shields with animal forms, hieroglyphic representations, and references to cipher-like practices involving signs and concealment.

entities.name: Cymric shields
entities.description: Shields associated with Cimmerians, called Timbres Casaubon by Gauls and Geregier by Germans.

entities.name: Tin Cask
entities.description: A symbolic container referenced as a cipher or concealment device, possibly representing hidden meaning.

entities.name: Proteus
entities.description: King of Egypt, transformed into various shapes by poets to assume different forms.
entities.quote: Proteus, the King of Egypt, was transformed by poets into all manner of shapes

entities.name: Diodorus Siculus
entities.description: Historian who records ancient customs, including Gaulish military practices and animal symbolism.
entities.quote: custom of the ancient Gauls in the army is attested by Diodorus Siculus

entities.name: Vegetius
entities.description: Roman military writer who mentions Roman sign-bearers using symbolic devices.
entities.quote: among Roman sign-bearers, as found in Vegetius

entities.name: Anubis
entities.description: Egyptian deity represented as a dog, cited as an example of symbolic transformation.

entities.name: Macedon
entities.description: Represented as a wolf in ancient symbolism, per Diodorus Siculus.

entities.name: Aristomenes
entities.description: King of the Messenians, depicted with a ram and wings in symbolic form.
entities.quote: Aristomenes, king of the Messenians, a ram with wings

entities.name: Pegasus
entities.description: Mythical winged horse referenced in connection with heraldic or symbolic imagery.

entities.name: Scytale
entities.description: A cryptographic device mentioned as being wrapped with a cord, possibly used for secret writing.
